name: German Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Ice water (or up to 3 tablespoons)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the flour and salt.
Cut in the shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in the vanilla extract.
2. Gradually add ice water, 1 tablespoon at a time, stirring until the pastry
holds together. Form into a ball.
3. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to an 1/8-inch thickness.
Transfer to a 9-inch pie plate. Trim and flute the edges.
4. In a separate bowl, combine the sugar, flour, and ground cinnamon for
filling.
5. Scatter about 3 tablespoons of the filling mixture into the crust.
6. Arrange half of the sliced apples on top of the filling. Sprinkle with
more of the remaining filling mixture.
7. Top with the remaining apples and sprinkle with the rest of the filling.
8. Pour heavy cream over the entire pie.
9. Bake at 450°F for about 20 minutes, then reduce heat to 350°F and bake for
an additional 35 to 40 minutes, or until the apples are tender and the
crust is golden brown.
10. Cool completely before serving. Serve with whipped cream, if desired.
Yield: 6-8 servings
